free_text: Activation analysis with a 9.1-MeV proton beam
The targets were made as metal disks 15 to 20-mm in
diameter, with thicknesses ensuring the total
absorption of protons. Samples of gaseous or chemically
active elements were prepared by pressing compounds of
these elements into aluminum cups.
All measurements were performed on samples having a
natural isotopic composition.
50-cm**3 volume Ge(Li)-detector 2.9-keV
resolution at 1332-keV of Co-60.
0.1 muA maximum proton current.
Time of sample irradiation - 20 minutes;
cooling time - 5 minutes;
time of activity measurement - 20 minutes.
The minimum cooling time was found to be 2
minutes.
For targets of complex chemical composition the
reaction yield from a pure element by making a
correction for the difference in stopping power.
The error of absolute gamma-ray yield
measurements was found to be less than 15%.
The major contribution to this error comes from
inaccuracy of the absolute detector efficiency.
For the activities with a half-life less than
irradiation time the measurement accuracy of this
origin may reach 10% for half-life < 5-min.
The uncertainty of beam energy.
-By authors.  In the analysis of the results we did not
take into account the gamma-ray attenuation in the
sample.  An appreciable attenuation is observable  only
for elements with Z > 50 at E(g) < 0.2 MeV.
Tbl.1 from JRN,89,447,1985
All Subents were corrected.
All REACTION codes are changed, according
to N.OTSUKA's remark.
Last checking has been done.

were corrected in Subent 027. ERR-ANALYS update.
